title: Age Peer Networking - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Age Peer Networking 9 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Mental Health Issues Older Adults Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BESTCARE TREATMENT SERVICES MADRAS MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES YOUNG ADULTS IN TRANSITION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support services for young adults that include transitional treatment and intervention to help them graduate from school, continue their education, find employment, maintain friendships, and gain independence. 850 SW 4th Street Suite 302 Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 who are living with mental health and/or behavioral challenges Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 475-6575 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BESTCARE TREATMENT SERVICES PRINEVILLE MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES YOUNG ADULTS IN TRANSITION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support services for young adults that include transitional treatment and intervention to help them graduate from school, continue their education, find employment, maintain friendships, and gain independence. 1059 NW Madras Highway Prineville, OR 97754 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 who are living with mental health and/or behavioral challenges Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 323-5330 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES SENIOR COMPANION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides volunteers to help with shopping, providing rides, and companionship. The program supports independence through shared activities, transportation, and companionship. Also provides short periods of relief to primary caregivers. Volunteer opportunities available. If income-eligible, volunteers may receive a small stipend plus mileage reimbursement. 2051 Kaen Road #135 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Program participants: Homebound older adults and adults with disabilities who meet all the following: Living independently in their home Ambulatory (cane or walker under 10 lbs.), no wheelchair or scooter bound Need transportation at least once a week Looking for weekly companionship and support Volunteers: Age 55 and older Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 7am-6pm Email (503) 655-8875 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COUNCIL ON AGING OF CENTRAL OREGON CARING CONNECTIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a social engagement program for seniors through a weekly phone call and/or in person. Trained volunteers are matched with individuals and are background checked. 1036 NE 5th Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Age 60 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 678-5483 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH YOUNG ADULTS IN TRANSITION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Young Adults in Transition (YAT) provides support services for young adults who suffer from mental health or behavioral challenges. YAT Teams establish relationships with young adults, families and peers, and all available support systems. Transitional treatment and intervention help young adults graduate school, continue education, find employment, maintain friendships, and gain independence. 2577 NE Courtney Drive Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 who are involved with the juvenile justice system, Department of Human Services currently homeless, or live in a foster home. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 213-6851 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH HILLSBORO RECOVERY CENTER CENTER FOR ADDICTIONS TRIAGE AND TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides access to organizations that offer substance use programs, including inpatient and outpatient treatment, and a peer drop-in center. 5250 NE Elam Young Parkway Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Adults experiencing substance use disorder issues Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm. Some services available evenings and weekends. (503) 846-2288 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YOUTH ERA COLUMBIA COUNTY VIRTUAL SERVICES PEER SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help, advice, guidance, and support from peer support specialists who have personal experience in youth-serving systems, including mental health, foster care, and juvenile justice. Services are community-based and can take place at individuals' homes or other location in the community, depending on the needs and preferences of the individual. PO Box 583 Eugene, OR 97440 Eligibility: Ages 12-25 Hours: Varies by service Email (971) 978-9797 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YOUTH ERA EUGENE DROP PEER SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help, advice, guidance, and support from peer support specialists who have personal experience in youth-serving systems, including mental health, foster care, and juvenile justice. Services are community-based and can take place at individuals' homes or other location in the community, depending on the needs and preferences of the individual. 44 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Ages 12-25 Hours: Varies by service Email (541) 517-9402 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YOUTH ERA SALEM DROP PEER SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help, advice, guidance, and support from peer support specialists who have personal experience in youth-serving systems, including mental health, foster care, and juvenile justice. Services are community-based and can take place at individuals' homes or other location in the community, depending on the needs and preferences of the individual. 246 State Street Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 12-25 Hours: Varies by service Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details BESTCARE TREATMENT SERVICES YOUNG ADULTS IN TRANSITION BESTCARE TREATMENT SERVICES YOUNG ADULTS IN TRANSITION CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES SENIOR COMPANION PROGRAM COUNCIL ON AGING OF CENTRAL OREGON CARING CONNECTIONS DESCHUTES COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH YOUNG ADULTS IN TRANSITION WASHINGTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH CENTER FOR ADDICTIONS TRIAGE AND TREATMENT YOUTH ERA PEER SUPPORT YOUTH ERA PEER SUPPORT YOUTH ERA PEER SUPPORT
